podcasts.name: Lenny's Podcast
podcasts.subtitle: Product | Career | Growth
podcasts.blurb: 面向产品和创业从业者，聚焦增长策略、职业发展与顶尖实践。

episodes.title: Why cultivating agency matters more than cultivating skills in the AI era | Max Schoening (Head of Product, Notion)
episodes.description:
Max Schoening is head of product at Notion, where he’s been especially effective at getting designers and PMs to ship code, prototype in the terminal, and launch extremely successful AI products. He was previously a PM at Google, ran design at Heroku, was VP of Design (and a part-time engineer) at GitHub, and is a two-time founder. He’s one of the most AI-forward product leaders out there and one of the deepest thinkers on how AI changes how we build and use software.
We discuss:
1. What’s most worked in getting designers and PMs to embrace AI
2. Why agency—not skills—is the thing that separates people who thrive from those who fall behind
3. How the first 10% of every project is now “free,” and what that means for product development
4. Max’s “tiny core” theory of great products: iPhone multitouch, the GitHub pull request, Notion blocks, Dropbox’s menu bar icon
5. Why the SaaSpocalypse is overstated
6. Why the amount of software has exploded but the quality hasn’t, and why that gap creates opportunity
episodes.descriptionCN:
Max Schoening 是 Notion 的产品主管，他在让设计师和产品经理在终端中发布代码、原型以及推出极其成功的人工智能产品方面特别有效。他曾担任 Google 的产品经理、Heroku 的设计主管、GitHub 的设计副总裁（兼兼职工程师），并且是两次创始人。他是人工智能最前沿的产品领导者之一，也是人工智能如何改变我们构建和使用软件方式最深刻的思考者之一。
我们讨论：
1. 对于让设计师和产品经理拥抱人工智能最有效的是什么
2. 为什么成功者与落后者的区别在于能动性而非技能
3. 每个项目的前 10% 现在如何“免费”，以及这对产品开发意味着什么
4. Max 伟大产品的“微小核心”理论：iPhone 多点触控、GitHub Pull Request、Notion 块、Dropbox 的菜单栏图标
5. 为什么 SaaS 末日被夸大了
6. 为什么软件数量激增但质量却没有，以及为什么这种差距创造了机会

episodes.title: Snapchat CEO: Why distribution has become the most important moat | Evan Spiegel
episodes.description:
Evan Spiegel, the co-founder and CEO of Snap, is one of the very few people in the world who has successfully built and scaled a lasting consumer social product. Snapchat has nearly 1 billion MAUs, and Evan and his team invented some of the most important consumer products and features, including Stories, AR glasses, swipe-based navigation, the camera as the primary UX, and a lot more.
In our in-depth conversation, we discuss:
1. Why distribution is now the biggest challenge for creating a consumer technology business
2. How Snap innovates at scale with a 9-to-12-person design team: no titles, no hierarchy, hundreds of ideas reviewed weekly with the CEO
3. Why a pure software business is no longer a moat, and what actually creates durable competitive advantages today
4. How AI is changing the way designers work and why they’re now shipping code
5. Why every major Snap feature was copied and how that forced the company to work differently
6. Evan’s prediction that humanity’s comfort with AI will be a bigger bottleneck than the technology itself
7. This year’s crucible moment for Snap
episodes.descriptionCN:
Snap 联合创始人兼首席执行官埃文·斯皮格尔 (Evan Spiegel) 是世界上极少数成功打造并扩展持久消费社交产品的人之一。 Snapchat 拥有近 10 亿月活跃用户，Evan 和他的团队发明了一些最重要的消费产品和功能，包括故事、AR 眼镜、基于滑动的导航、作为主要用户体验的相机等等。
在我们的深入对话中，我们讨论：
1. 为什么分销现在是创建消费技术业务的最大挑战
2. Snap 如何通过 9 到 12 人的设计团队进行大规模创新：没有头衔，没有等级��度，每周与首席执行官一起审查数百个想法
3. 为什么纯软件业务不再是护城河，以及当今真正创造持久竞争优势的因素
4. 人工智能如何改变设计师的工作方式以及他们现在交付代码的原因
5. 为什么 Snap 的每一个主要功能都被复制，这如何迫使公司以不同的方式工作
6. 埃文预测人类对人工智能的舒适度将是比技术本身更大的瓶颈
7. 今年 Snap 的严��时刻

episodes.title: How Anthropic’s product team moves faster than anyone else | Cat Wu (Head of Product, Claude Code)
episodes.description:
Cat Wu is Head of Product for Claude Code and Cowork at Anthropic, building one of the most important AI products of this generation. Before joining Anthropic, Cat spent years as an engineer and briefly worked in VC. Today, she’s interviewing hundreds of product managers who are trying to break into AI—and seeing firsthand what separates those who thrive from those who fall behind.
We discuss:
1. How Anthropic’s shipping cadence went from months to weeks to days
2. The emerging skills PMs need to develop right now
3. Why you need to build products that don’t yet fully work, so you’re ready when the next model closes the gap
4. Cat’s most underrated AI skill: asking the model to introspect on its own mistakes
5. Why Claude’s personality is core to its success
6. Why Anthropic’s mission alignment eliminates the friction that slows most large organizations
7. Why “just do things” is the most important principle for working at AI-native companies
episodes.descriptionCN:
Cat Wu 是 Anthropic 的 Claude Code 和 Cowork 的产品主管，构建了这一代最重要的人工智能产品之一。在加入 Anthropic 之前，Cat 担任了多年工程师，并曾短暂从事过风险投资工作。今天，她正在采访数百名试图进军人工智能领域的产品经理，并亲眼目睹是什么将成功者与落后者区分开来。
我们讨论：
1. Anthropic 的发货节奏如何从几个月缩短到几周甚至几天
2. PM 现在需要发展的新兴技能
3. 为什么您需要构建尚未完全发挥作用的产品，以便在下一个模型缩小差距时做好准备
4. Cat最被低估的AI技能：要求模型反思自己的错误
5. 为什么克劳德的个性是其成功的核心
6. 为什么 Anthropic 的使命一致性消除了阻碍大多数大型组织发展的摩擦
7. 为什么“只做事”是人工智能原生公司最重要的工作原则

episodes.title: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
episodes.description:
Nikhyl Singhal is the founder of The Skip, a community for senior product leaders; a former product exec at Meta, Google, and Credit Karma; and a many-time founder. He’s also one of the most honest, unfiltered voices on what’s actually happening in product management right now.
In our in-depth conversation, we discuss:
1. Why the next two years will be the most chaotic period in product management history
2. Why half of current product managers are at risk, and what separates those who’ll do well
3. Why you need to find your “moments of joy” with AI
4. The “smiling exhaustion” he’s seeing across the product community
5. The psychological barriers that prevent people from reinventing themselves
6. Why your resume’s fancy logos matter less than ever, and what matters now
7. His prediction that companies will shed 30,000 people and rehire 8,000—all AI-first
episodes.descriptionCN:
Nikhyl Singhal 是 The Skip 的创始人，The Skip 是一个高级产品领导者社区； Meta、Google 和 Credit Karma 的前产品主管；以及多次创始人。对于目前产品管理中实际发生的情况，他也是最诚实、最未经过滤的声音之一。
在我们的深入对话中，我们讨论：
1. 为什么未来两年将是产品管理史上最混乱的时期
2. 为什么现任产品经理有一半面临风险，以及那些表现出色的产品经理的区别
3. 为什么你需要用人工智能来寻找你的“快乐时刻”
4. 他在整个产品社区中看到的“微笑的疲惫”
5.阻碍人们重塑自我的心理障碍
6. 为什么你的简历中花哨的标志比以往任何时候都不再重要，而现在什么才是重要的
7. 他预测公司将裁员 30,000 人并重新雇用 8,000 人——所有人工智能优先

episodes.title: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
episodes.description:
Keith Rabois was an early executive at PayPal (part of the famous PayPal Mafia), COO at Square, VP of Corporate Development at LinkedIn, and an early investor in Stripe, DoorDash, Airbnb, YouTube, Ramp, and Palantir. Currently he’s managing director at Khosla Ventures. Also, he hasn’t touched a computer since September 2010 (he does everything from an iPad).
In our in-depth conversation, Keith shares:
1. The barrels vs. ammunition hiring framework (and how to spot barrels)
2. Why talking to customers is actively harmful for consumer products
3. How to identify undiscovered talent
4. Why the PM role is dying
5. The three traits of the best-performing companies right now
6. The specific interview question he asks every senior candidate
7. Why CMOs (not engineers) are becoming the #1 consumer of tokens
episodes.descriptionCN:
Keith Rabois 是 PayPal（著名的 PayPal Mafia 的一部分）的早期高管、Square 的首席运营官、LinkedIn 的企业发展副总裁，以及 Stripe、DoorDash、Airbnb、YouTube、Ramp 和 Palantir 的早期投资者。目前，他担任科斯拉风险投资公司 (Khosla Ventures) 的董事总经理。此外，自 2010 年 9 月以来，他就没有碰过电脑（他所有的事情都通过 iPad 进行）。
在我们的深入对话中，基思分享了：
1. 枪管与弹药租赁框架（以及如何发现枪管）
2. 为什么与客户交谈对消费品有害
3. 如何识别未被发现的人才
4. 为什么 PM 角色正在消亡
5. 目前表现最好的公司的三个特征
6. 他向每一位资深候选人提出的具体面试问题
7. 为什么 CMO（而不是工程师）正在成为代币的第一大消费者

episodes.title: Head of Growth (Anthropic): “Claude is growing itself at this point” | Amol Avasare
episodes.description:
Amol Avasare is Head of Growth at Anthropic, which is going through the most unprecedented growth trajectory in history—scaling from $1 billion to over $19 billion in ARR in just 14 months. Previously, Amol worked on the growth teams at Mercury and MasterClass. Before that he was a founder, and he cold emailed his way into the Anthropic role when no job listing existed. Most remarkably, he overcame a traumatic brain injury from a Muay Thai match that meant he couldn't work for nearly a year.
In our in-depth discussion, Amol shares:
1. How Amol landed his role by cold emailing Anthropic’s CPO Mike Krieger
2. How Anthropic is automating growth experiments with Claude (their internal tool called “CASH”)
3. Why the ratio of PMs to engineers might need to flip (more PMs than engineers) as AI makes engineers exponentially more productive
4. Why activation is the single highest-leverage growth problem in AI
5. Why Anthropic indexes 70/30 toward big bets (the opposite of most growth teams)
6. How he uses Cowork to detect team misalignment in Slack
7. How the company’s focus on AI coding created a research flywheel that accelerated their models
episodes.descriptionCN:
Amol Avasare 是 Anthropic 的增长主管，该公司正在经历历史上最前所未有的增长轨迹——ARR 在短短 14 个月内从 10 亿美元扩大到超过 190 亿美元。此前，Amol 曾在 Mercury 和 MasterClass 的增长团队工作。在此之前，他是一名创始人，在没有职位空缺的情况下，他通过冷冰冰的电子邮件进入了人类角色。最引人注目的是，他克服了泰拳比赛造成的脑外伤，这意味着他近一年无法工作。
在我们的深入讨论中，Amol 分享了：
1. Amol 是如何通过向 Anthropic 的 CPO Mike Krieger 发送冷邮件而获得职位的
2. Anthropic 如何与 Claude 一起自动化生长实验（他们的内部工具称为“CASH”）
3. 为什么随着人工智能使工程师的工作效率成倍提高，PM 与工程师的比例可能需要调整（PM 的数量多于工程师）
4. 为什么激活是人工智能中杠杆率最高的单一增长问题
5. 为什么 Anthropic 对大赌注的指数为 70/30（与大多数增长团队相反）
6. 他如何使用 Cowork 来检测 Slack 中的团队失调
7. 该公司对人工智能编码的关注如何创建了一个研究飞轮来加速他们的模型

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.subtitle: AI News | Strategy | Industry
podcasts.blurb: 每日 AI 动态速览，覆盖模型进展、政策变化与商业影响。

episodes.title: The Week AI Grew Up
episodes.description:
Transition from startup-style AI to infrastructure-era AI marked by explosive token demand, compute bottlenecks, and a shift to usage-based pricing.
Market signals include roughly 40% higher GPU rental costs, record cloud growth at major providers, and widespread adoption of per-use billing to manage token scarcity. Product and policy developments highlight agent and harness platforms such as Cursor and Codex, government scrutiny of supply‑chain and deployment risk, and anomalous model behaviors that are prompting new audit tools.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
从初创型人工智能向基础设施时代人工智能的转变，其特点是爆炸性的代币需求、计算瓶颈以及向基于使用的定价的转变。
市场信号包括 GPU 租赁成本增加约 40%、主要提供商创纪录的云增长以及广泛采用按使用计费来管理代币稀缺。产品和政策的发展突出了 Cursor 和 Codex 等代理和工具平台、政府对供应链和部署风险的审查，以及催生新审计工具的异常模型行为。
AI 每日简报可帮助您了解 AI 领域最重要的新闻和讨论。

episodes.title: How Harness-as-a-Service Will Change Agents
episodes.description:
Big tech earnings show an AI-driven cloud surge and soaring compute spending across Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta. Harness-as-a-service emerges as a new infrastructure layer, with cursor SDK, managed agents, and hosted runtimes providing sandboxed execution environments. Benchmarks and demos reveal large performance gains from harness improvements and rapid growth of agentic apps for coding, IT triage, and workflow automation.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.

episodes.title: The AI Subsidy Era is Over
episodes.description:
End of the AI subsidy era forces a shift from flat-fee subscriptions to usage-based pricing as agentic, token-heavy workflows escalate inference costs.
Anthropic, OpenAI, Microsoft, and GitHub Copilot reveal capacity constraints, model metering, and steep price multipliers with direct impacts on vendor competition and market expectations. Practical strategies include auditing AI spending, running cheap-model bake-offs, appointing a model sommelier, building escalation paths, and maintaining an AI cost scoreboard.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
人工智能补贴时代的结束迫使人们从固定费用订阅转向基于使用的定价，因为代理、代币密集的工作流程会增加推理成本。
Anthropic、OpenAI、微软和 GitHub Copilot 揭示了容量限制、模型计量和陡峭的价格乘数，对供应商竞争和市场预期产生直接影响。实用策略包括审计人工智能支出、运行廉价模型烘焙比赛、任命模型侍酒师、建立升级路径以及维护人工智能成本记分板。

episodes.title: AI Lab Power Rankings
episodes.description:
Microsoft and OpenAI amended the partnership to remove cloud exclusivity, allowing OpenAI models on AWS while preserving Microsoft's equity stake and a long-term revenue share. Power rankings evaluate labs across compute, enterprise positioning, platform control, consumer reach, model strength, momentum, branded narrative, and X factor, with Google, OpenAI, and Microsoft leading the list. Key themes include the shift to an agentic era, looming token and compute shortages, the rise of desktop agents like Amazon Quick, and rapidly changing competitive strategies across Anthropic, Meta, Apple, Amazon, and X.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.

episodes.descriptionCN:
微软和 OpenAI 修改了合作伙伴关系，取消了云排他性，允许 OpenAI 在 AWS 上运行模型，同时保留微软的股权和长期收入份额。实力排名从计算、企业定位、平台控制、消费者影响力、模型强度、势头、品牌叙事和 X 因素等方面对实验室进行评估，其中 Google、OpenAI 和 Microsoft 名列前茅。关键主题包括向代理时代的转变、迫在眉睫的代币和计算短缺、Amazon Quick 等桌面代理的崛起，以及 Anthropic、Meta、Apple、Amazon 和 X 之间快速变化的竞争策略。

episodes.title: How Deepseek v4 Connects to the US Grid
episodes.description:
Google's reported $40 billion investment into Anthropic and hyperscaler deals with Broadcom, Amazon, and Microsoft lock down gigawatts of compute for AI training. White House invocation of the Defense Production Act to bolster US electric‑grid infrastructure spotlights energy and supply‑chain constraints, creating a potential bottleneck for AI growth and upward pressure on electricity prices. DeepSeek V4 (PRO and Flash) debuts with million‑token context windows and pricing far below leading Western models, shifting attention toward affordable, deployable models over pure frontier performance.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.

episodes.descriptionCN:
据报道，谷歌已与博通、亚马逊和微软达成协议，向 Anthropic 和超大规模公司投资 400 亿美元，锁定用于人工智能训练的千兆瓦计算能力。白宫援引《国防生产法》来加强美国电网基础设施，凸显了能源和供应链的限制，为人工智能的增长造成了潜在的瓶颈，并给电价带来了上行压力。 DeepSeek V4（PRO 和 Flash）以百万代币上下文窗口首次亮相，价格远低于领先的西方模型，将注意力转向经济实惠、可部署的模型，而不是纯粹的前沿性能。

episodes.title: Where the Economy Thrives After AI
episodes.description:
A new economic argument suggests AI won’t simply wipe out work, but will shift value toward the parts of the economy where human presence, provenance, care, taste, and relationship matter most. NLW explores Alex Imas' case for a post-commodity economy, why automation may make relational work more valuable, and how the AI jobs debate is missing the question of what new demand gets unlocked when supply becomes abundant.
Source: ⁠
SIGN UP FOR OUR NEW FREE PROGRAM: AGENTOS

episodes.descriptionCN:
一项新的经济论点表明，人工智能不会简单地消灭工作，而是会将价值转移到人类存在、出身、关怀、品味和关系最重要的经济领域。 NLW 探讨了 Alex Imas 的后商品经济案例，为什么自动化可能使关系性工作更有价值，以及人工智能就业辩论如何忽略了当供应变得充足时会释放哪些新需求的问题。

podcasts.name: Dwarkesh Patel
podcasts.subtitle: AI | Economics | Science
podcasts.blurb: 与研究者和思想者深聊 AI、经济与科技长期趋势。

episodes.title: The Trillion-Dollar Timing Problem in AI
What Is the Pentagon's Plan With Anthropic?
Neural Networks Are Cryptography in Reverse - Reiner Pope
Why the Nukes Analogy for AI Is Wrong
The Man Who Saved the World by Disobeying and What It Means for AI
How GPT, Claude, and Gemini are actually trained and served – Reiner Pope
episodes.description:
Did a very different format with Reiner Pope – a blackboard lecture where he walks through how frontier LLMs are trained and served. It's shocking how much you can deduce about what the labs are doing from a handful of equations, public API prices, and some chalk. It’s a bit technical, but I encourage you to hang in there - it’s really worth it.
There are less than a handful of people who understand the full stack of AI, from chip design to model architecture, as well as Reiner. It was a real delight to learn from him.
Reiner is CEO of MatX, a new chip startup (full disclosure - I’m an angel investor). He was previously at Google, where he worked on software efficiency, compilers, and TPU architecture.
Wrote up some flashcards and practice problems to help myself retain what Reiner taught. Hope it's helpful to you too!
Download markdown of transcript here to chat with an LLM:
0:00:00 – How batch size affects token cost and speed
0:31:59 – How MoE models are laid out across GPU racks
0:47:02 – How pipeline parallelism spreads model layers across racks
1:03:27 – Why Ilya said, “As we now know, pipelining is not wise.”
1:18:49 – Because of RL, models may be 100x over-trained beyond Chinchilla-optimal
1:32:52 – Deducing long context memory costs from API pricing
2:03:52 – Convergent evolution between neural nets and cryptography
episodes.descriptionCN:
与 Reiner Pope 进行了一种截然不同的形式——黑板讲座，他介绍了前沿法学硕士的培训和服务方式。令人震惊的是，你可以从一些方程、公共 API 价格和一些粉笔中推断出实验室正在做的事情。这有点技术性，但我鼓励你坚持下去——这真的很值得。
像 Reiner 一样，了解从芯片设计到模型架构的整个人工智能堆栈的人并不多。向他学习真的很高兴。
Reiner 是新芯片初���公司 MatX 的首席执行官（全面披露 - 我是天使投资人）。他之前在 Google 工作，负责软件效率、编译器和 TPU 架构。
写了一些抽认卡和练习题来帮助自己记住莱纳所教的内容。希望对您也有帮助！
在此处下载成绩单的降价版本以与法学硕士聊天：
0:00:00 – 批量大小如何影响代币成本和速度
0:31:59 – MoE 模型如何跨 GPU 机架布局
0:47:02 – 管道并行性如何跨机架传播模型层
1:03:27 – 为什么 Ilya 说：“正如我们现在所知，流水线并不明智。”
1:18:49 – 由于强化学习，模型可能会过度训练 100 倍，超出 Chinchilla 最优值
1:32:52 – 从 API 定价中推导出长上下文内存成本
2:03:52 – 神经网络和密码学之间的趋同进化

episodes.descriptionCN: 我和 Ras Mic 一起坐下来分析人工智能代理的实际工作原理以及为什么大多数人都错误地使用它们。 Ras Mic 解释了上下文窗口的机制，并举例说明了代理 md 文件...
